description.en: Why investing exists at all — beating inflation, the power of compounding, and the discipline of investing steadily over a long horizon.
description.ru: Зачем вообще нужны инвестиции — как обгонять инфляцию, использовать силу сложного процента и дисциплинированно инвестировать на длинном горизонте.
description.kk: Инвестиция не үшін қажет: инфляциядан озу, күрделі өсімнің күшін пайдалану және ұзақ мерзімде тұрақты инвестиция жасау тәртібі.

description.en: A savings account feels safe, but if its rate trails inflation, your money quietly shrinks in real terms every year.
description.ru: Сберегательный счёт кажется безопасным, но если его ставка ниже инфляции, деньги каждый год незаметно уменьшаются в реальном выражении.
description.kk: Жинақ шоты қауіпсіз көрінеді, бірақ оның мөлшерлемесі инфляциядан төмен болса, ақшаңыз жыл сайын нақты мәнде баяу азаяды.

title.ru: Безопасный счёт, который не так уж безопасен
title.kk: Қауіпсіз сияқты, бірақ толық қауіпсіз емес шот

body.en: Putting money in a savings account feels responsible. But safety is measured in numbers on a screen, not in what those numbers can buy. When prices rise faster than your interest, the account shrinks in real terms even as the balance grows.
body.ru: Держать деньги на сберегательном счёте кажется разумным. Но безопасность измеряется не цифрами на экране, а тем, что эти цифры могут купить. Когда цены растут быстрее, чем начисляются проценты, счёт уменьшается в реальном выражении, даже если баланс растёт.
body.kk: Ақшаны жинақ шотында ұстау жауапты шешім сияқты. Бірақ қауіпсіздік экрандағы санмен емес, сол санның не сатып ала алатынымен өлшенеді. Бағалар пайыздан жылдам өссе, баланс көбейгенімен, шот нақты мәнде азаяды.

tip.en: The question isn't 'Is my balance growing?' It's 'Is my balance growing faster than prices?'
tip.ru: Вопрос не в том, растёт ли баланс. Вопрос в том, растёт ли он быстрее цен.
tip.kk: The question isn't 'Is my balance growing?' It's 'Is my balance growing faster than prices?'

title.en: Purchasing power: what money actually buys
title.ru: Покупательная способность: что деньги реально могут купить
title.kk: Сатып алу қабілеті: ақша шын мәнінде не сатып алады

body.en: Inflation is the rate at which the general price level rises. If inflation is 9% and your savings account pays 7%, your real return is roughly −2%. You end the year with more tenge but less purchasing power — you can buy less than you could 12 months ago.
body.ru: Инфляция — это темп роста общего уровня цен. Если инфляция составляет 9%, а сберегательный счёт даёт 7%, ваша реальная доходность примерно равна −2%. В конце года у вас больше тенге, но меньше покупательная способность — вы можете купить меньше, чем 12 месяцев назад.
body.kk: Инфляция — жалпы баға деңгейінің өсу қарқыны. Егер инфляция 9%, ал жинақ шоты 7% төлесе, нақты табыстылық шамамен −2% болады. Жыл соңында теңге көбірек, бірақ сатып алу қабілеті төмен болады — 12 ай бұрынғыдан аз сатып аласыз.

example.en: 500,000 ₸ at 7% becomes 535,000 ₸ after one year. But if prices rose 9%, the same basket of goods now costs 545,000 ₸. Your real loss: ~10,000 ₸.
example.ru: 500 000 ₸ под 7% через год превращаются в 535 000 ₸. Но если цены выросли на 9%, та же корзина товаров теперь стоит 545 000 ₸. Реальная потеря: около 10 000 ₸.
example.kk: 500 000 ₸ 7% мөлшерлемемен бір жылдан кейін 535 000 ₸ болады. Бірақ бағалар 9% өссе, сол тауар себеті енді 545 000 ₸ тұрады. Нақты жоғалту: шамамен 10 000 ₸.

body.en: A 2% annual real loss sounds small. Over 10 years it cuts purchasing power by about 18%. The effect is invisible year to year but unmistakable over a decade. This is not a crisis — it is a slow, quiet drain that only investing can offset.
body.ru: Реальная потеря 2% в год звучит небольшой. Но за 10 лет она снижает покупательную способность примерно на 18%. Из года в год эффект почти незаметен, но за десятилетие он очевиден. Это не кризис, а медленная тихая утечка, которую могут компенсировать только инвестиции.
body.kk: Жылына 2% нақты шығын аз сияқты. Бірақ 10 жылда ол сатып алу қабілетін шамамен 18% азайтады. Жыл сайын байқалмауы мүмкін, бірақ он жылда әсері анық көрінеді. Бұл дағдарыс емес — инвестиция ғана өтей алатын баяу, үнсіз азаю.

tip.en: Inflation is not the enemy of saving. It is the cost of not investing.
tip.ru: Инфляция не враг сбережений. Это цена отказа от инвестирования.
tip.kk: Инфляция жинақтың жауы емес. Ол инвестиция жасамаудың құны.

blocks.text: Assume inflation averages 8% per year. Here is what happens to 1,000,000 ₸ depending on where it sits.
Only investing at a rate above inflation results in genuine growth of purchasing power.
Предположим, инфляция в среднем составляет 8% в год. Вот что происходит с 1 000 000 ₸ в зависимости от того, где лежат деньги.
Только инвестиции с доходностью выше инфляции дают настоящий рост покупательной способности.
Assume inflation averages 8% per year. Here is what happens to 1,000,000 ₸ depending on where it sits.
Only investing at a rate above inflation results in genuine growth of purchasing power.

en.instruction: Inflation is 9% this year. For each situation, decide whether the person's real purchasing power increased, stayed flat, or decreased.
options: Real gain
Roughly flat
Real loss

scenarios.text: Asel earned 11% on her bond fund while inflation was 9%.
scenarios.explanation: 11% nominal minus 9% inflation = roughly +2% real return. Her purchasing power grew.

scenarios.text: Daulet kept 800,000 ₸ in a current account paying 0% for the year.
scenarios.explanation: 0% nominal minus 9% inflation = −9% real. Daulet can buy about 9% less with that money now.

scenarios.text: Mira's savings account paid exactly 9% — matching inflation.
scenarios.explanation: When nominal return equals inflation, real return is approximately zero. Purchasing power held steady.

ru.instruction: Инфляция в этом году — 9%. Для каждой ситуации определите, выросла ли реальная покупательная способность человека, осталась примерно прежней или снизилась.
options: Реальный прирост
Примерно без изменений
Реальная потеря

scenarios.text: Асель заработала 11% на облигационном фонде при инфляции 9%.
scenarios.explanation: 11% номинальной доходности минус 9% инфляции = примерно +2% реальной доходности. Её покупательная способность выросла.

scenarios.text: Даулет держал 800 000 ₸ на текущем счёте с доходностью 0% в течение года.
scenarios.explanation: 0% номинально минус 9% инфляции = −9% реально. Теперь Даулет может купить на эти деньги примерно на 9% меньше.

scenarios.text: Сберегательный счёт Миры дал ровно 9% — столько же, сколько инфляция.
scenarios.explanation: Когда номинальная доходность равна инфляции, реальная доходность примерно равна нулю. Покупательная способность сохранилась.

kk.instruction: Inflation is 9% this year. For each situation, decide whether the person's real purchasing power increased, stayed flat, or decreased.
options: Нақты өсім
Шамамен өзгеріссіз
Нақты шығын

scenarios.text: Asel earned 11% on her bond fund while inflation was 9%.
scenarios.explanation: 11% nominal minus 9% inflation = roughly +2% real return. Her purchasing power grew.

scenarios.text: Daulet kept 800,000 ₸ in a current account paying 0% for the year.
scenarios.explanation: 0% nominal minus 9% inflation = −9% real. Daulet can buy about 9% less with that money now.

scenarios.text: Mira's savings account paid exactly 9% — matching inflation.
scenarios.explanation: When nominal return equals inflation, real return is approximately zero. Purchasing power held steady.

title.en: Investing is not optional — it is the baseline
title.ru: Инвестирование — не опция, а базовый уровень
title.kk: Investing is not optional — it is the baseline

body.en: Saving preserves the number; investing preserves the value. In an economy with persistent inflation, not investing is itself a financial decision — one with a predictable, negative real outcome. The next question is how returns actually compound over time.
body.ru: Сбережения сохраняют число; инвестиции сохраняют ценность. В экономике с устойчивой инфляцией отказ от инвестирования сам по себе является финансовым решением — с предсказуемым отрицательным реальным результатом. Следующий вопрос — как доходность накапливается со временем.
body.kk: Saving preserves the number; investing preserves the value. In an economy with persistent inflation, not investing is itself a financial decision — one with a predictable, negative real outcome. The next question is how returns actually compound over time.

tip.en: Target a real return: nominal return minus expected inflation. That is your actual progress.
tip.ru: Ориентируйтесь на реальную доходность: номинальная доходность минус ожидаемая инфляция. Это и есть ваш настоящий прогресс.
tip.kk: Target a real return: nominal return minus expected inflation. That is your actual progress.

question_text.en: A savings account yields 8%. Inflation this year is 10%. Which statement best describes what happened to the account holder's wealth?
question_text.ru: Сберегательный счёт приносит 8%. Инфляция в этом году — 10%. Какое утверждение лучше всего описывает, что произошло с богатством владельца счёта?
question_text.kk: A savings account yields 8%. Inflation this year is 10%. Which statement best describes what happened to the account holder's wealth?

answer_text.en: Their purchasing power fell by roughly 2%, even though the nominal balance grew
answer_text.ru: Их покупательная способность снизилась примерно на 2%, хотя номинальный баланс вырос
answer_text.kk: Their purchasing power fell by roughly 2%, even though the nominal balance grew

answer_text.en: Their wealth grew by 8% because the account balance increased
answer_text.ru: Их богатство выросло на 8%, потому что баланс счёта увеличился
answer_text.kk: Their wealth grew by 8% because the account balance increased

answer_text.en: They broke even because interest partially offset inflation
answer_text.ru: Они вышли в ноль, потому что проценты частично компенсировали инфляцию
answer_text.kk: They broke even because interest partially offset inflation

answer_text.en: Their purchasing power is unchanged; inflation only affects prices, not savings
answer_text.ru: Их покупательная способность не изменилась; инфляция влияет только на цены, а не на сбережения
answer_text.kk: Their purchasing power is unchanged; inflation only affects prices, not savings

question_text.en: Over 15 years, a person keeps all savings in cash. Inflation averaged 7% annually. Roughly what fraction of their original purchasing power remains?
question_text.ru: Человек 15 лет держит все сбережения наличными. Инфляция в среднем составляла 7% в год. Какая примерно доля первоначальной покупательной способности сохранится?
question_text.kk: Over 15 years, a person keeps all savings in cash. Inflation averaged 7% annually. Roughly what fraction of their original purchasing power remains?

answer_text.en: About 36% — less than half
answer_text.ru: Около 36% — меньше половины
answer_text.kk: About 36% — less than half

answer_text.en: About 70% — a modest decline
answer_text.ru: Около 70% — умеренное снижение
answer_text.kk: About 70% — a modest decline

answer_text.en: 100% — cash does not lose value if you don't spend it
answer_text.ru: 100% — наличные не теряют ценность, если их не тратить
answer_text.kk: 100% — cash does not lose value if you don't spend it

answer_text.en: About 85% — inflation has a small effect over 15 years
answer_text.ru: Около 85% — инфляция за 15 лет влияет слабо
answer_text.kk: About 85% — inflation has a small effect over 15 years

description.en: Returns that generate their own returns. The earlier you start, the less capital you need to reach the same result.
description.ru: Доходность, которая создаёт новую доходность. Чем раньше вы начинаете, тем меньше капитала нужно для того же результата.
description.kk: Returns that generate their own returns. The earlier you start, the less capital you need to reach the same result.

body.en: Simple interest pays you a fixed amount on your original principal each year. Compounding does something more powerful: each year's gains are added to the base, so the next year's gains are calculated on a larger number. You earn returns on your returns.
body.ru: Простые проценты каждый год платят фиксированную сумму на первоначальный капитал. Сложный процент делает больше: прибыль каждого года добавляется к базе, поэтому прибыль следующего года считается уже от большей суммы. Вы получаете доход на уже полученный доход.
body.kk: Simple interest pays you a fixed amount on your original principal each year. Compounding does something more powerful: each year's gains are added to the base, so the next year's gains are calculated on a larger number. You earn returns on your returns.

tip.en: Compounding is not a feature of a product. It is a mathematical property of reinvested returns.
tip.ru: Сложный процент — не характеристика продукта. Это математическое свойство реинвестированной доходности.
tip.kk: Compounding is not a feature of a product. It is a mathematical property of reinvested returns.

body.en: In the early years, compounding adds modest sums. But the base keeps growing, so each year's increment is larger than the last. After enough time, the returns on past returns dwarf the original contribution. This makes starting early more powerful than starting big.
body.ru: В первые годы сложный процент добавляет небольшие суммы. Но база постоянно растёт, поэтому прирост каждого следующего года больше предыдущего. Через достаточно долгое время доходы на прошлые доходы становятся больше первоначального взноса. Поэтому начать рано важнее, чем начать с большой суммы.
body.kk: In the early years, compounding adds modest sums. But the base keeps growing, so each year's increment is larger than the last. After enough time, the returns on past returns dwarf the original contribution. This makes starting early more powerful than starting big.

example.en: Investing 100,000 ₸ at 10% for 30 years gives ~1,745,000 ₸. Waiting 10 years and investing the same 100,000 ₸ for only 20 years gives ~673,000 ₸ — less than 40% as much, despite investing the same sum.
example.ru: Инвестиция 100 000 ₸ под 10% на 30 лет даёт около 1 745 000 ₸. Если подождать 10 лет и вложить те же 100 000 ₸ только на 20 лет, получится около 673 000 ₸ — меньше 40% от первого результата, хотя сумма вложения та же.
example.kk: Investing 100,000 ₸ at 10% for 30 years gives ~1,745,000 ₸. Waiting 10 years and investing the same 100,000 ₸ for only 20 years gives ~673,000 ₸ — less than 40% as much, despite investing the same sum.

body.en: Compounding can happen annually, monthly, or daily. More frequent compounding produces slightly more growth because reinvestment happens sooner. For most long-term investors, annual vs monthly compounding makes a small but real difference over decades.
body.ru: Начисление может происходить ежегодно, ежемесячно или ежедневно. Более частое начисление даёт немного больший рост, потому что реинвестирование происходит раньше. Для большинства долгосрочных инвесторов разница между ежегодным и ежемесячным начислением невелика, но за десятилетия она становится реальной.
body.kk: Compounding can happen annually, monthly, or daily. More frequent compounding produces slightly more growth because reinvestment happens sooner. For most long-term investors, annual vs monthly compounding makes a small but real difference over decades.

tip.en: For long horizons, frequency matters less than rate and time. Focus on those two first.
tip.ru: На длинном горизонте частота менее важна, чем ставка и время. Сначала сосредоточьтесь на них.
tip.kk: For long horizons, frequency matters less than rate and time. Focus on those two first.

blocks.text: Both invest 50,000 ₸/year at 10% annual return. One starts at 25, the other at 35. Both stop at 65.
The early starter contributed only 500,000 ₸ more but ends up with nearly 3× as much. The extra 10 years of compounding did more work than the extra capital.
Оба инвестируют по 50 000 ₸ в год под 10% годовых. Один начинает в 25 лет, другой — в 35. Оба останавливаются в 65.
Ранний инвестор внёс всего на 500 000 ₸ больше, но в итоге получил почти в 3 раза больше. Дополнительные 10 лет сложного процента сделали больше, чем дополнительный капитал.
Both invest 50,000 ₸/year at 10% annual return. One starts at 25, the other at 35. Both stop at 65.
The early starter contributed only 500,000 ₸ more but ends up with nearly 3× as much. The extra 10 years of compounding did more work than the extra capital.

title.en: Sort these into what compounding needs
title.kk: Sort these into what compounding needs

en.instruction: Drag each factor into the correct category: things that accelerate compounding vs things that slow or break it.
en.explanation: Compounding requires three things to work fully: reinvested returns, time, and a decent rate. Fees and withdrawals eat into the base, reducing what the next cycle compounds on.

ru.instruction: Перетащите каждый фактор в правильную категорию: что ускоряет сложный процент, а что замедляет или ломает его.
ru.explanation: Чтобы сложный процент работал в полную силу, нужны три вещи: реинвестированная доходность, время и достойная ставка. Комиссии и снятия уменьшают базу, на которую начисляется следующий цикл.

kk.instruction: Drag each factor into the correct category: things that accelerate compounding vs things that slow or break it.
kk.explanation: Compounding requires three things to work fully: reinvested returns, time, and a decent rate. Fees and withdrawals eat into the base, reducing what the next cycle compounds on.

title.en: The main lesson: time is the input you can't buy back
title.ru: Главный урок: время — ресурс, который нельзя вернуть
title.kk: The main lesson: time is the input you can't buy back

body.en: Compounding makes starting early worth more than starting rich. Every year of delay costs you the returns on all future returns that year would have generated. The Rule of 72 is the shortcut for estimating exactly how powerful this is for any given rate.
body.ru: Сложный процент делает ранний старт ценнее, чем большой стартовый капитал. Каждый год задержки стоит доходов на все будущие доходы, которые этот год мог бы создать. Правило 72 — быстрый способ оценить силу этого эффекта при любой ставке.
body.kk: Compounding makes starting early worth more than starting rich. Every year of delay costs you the returns on all future returns that year would have generated. The Rule of 72 is the shortcut for estimating exactly how powerful this is for any given rate.

tip.en: The best investment decision you can make today is not which asset to buy — it is not waiting to start.
tip.ru: Лучшее инвестиционное решение сегодня — не выбрать идеальный актив, а не откладывать старт.
tip.kk: The best investment decision you can make today is not which asset to buy — it is not waiting to start.

question_text.en: Investor A puts in 200,000 ₸ at age 20 and never adds more. Investor B puts in 200,000 ₸ at age 40. Both earn 10%/year until age 60. Who ends up with more, and why?
question_text.ru: Инвестор A вкладывает 200 000 ₸ в 20 лет и больше ничего не добавляет. Инвестор B вкладывает 200 000 ₸ в 40 лет. Оба получают 10% в год до 60 лет. У кого будет больше и почему?
question_text.kk: Investor A puts in 200,000 ₸ at age 20 and never adds more. Investor B puts in 200,000 ₸ at age 40. Both earn 10%/year until age 60. Who ends up with more, and why?

answer_text.en: A, because 40 years of compounding produces far more than 20 years on the same principal
answer_text.ru: У A, потому что 40 лет сложного процента дают гораздо больше, чем 20 лет на той же сумме
answer_text.kk: A, because 40 years of compounding produces far more than 20 years on the same principal

answer_text.en: B, because markets are more mature and predictable now
answer_text.ru: У B, потому что рынки сейчас более зрелые и предсказуемые
answer_text.kk: B, because markets are more mature and predictable now

answer_text.en: They end up equal since both invested the same amount
answer_text.ru: Они получат одинаково, потому что вложили одинаковую сумму
answer_text.kk: They end up equal since both invested the same amount

answer_text.en: A, but only marginally — the difference in time is small relative to the amount
answer_text.ru: У A, но ненамного — разница во времени мала по сравнению с суммой
answer_text.kk: A, but only marginally — the difference in time is small relative to the amount

question_text.en: An investor earns 10% annually but withdraws all returns each year instead of reinvesting. What does she lose compared to compounding?
question_text.ru: Инвестор получает 10% годовых, но каждый год снимает всю прибыль вместо реинвестирования. Что она теряет по сравнению со сложным процентом?
question_text.kk: An investor earns 10% annually but withdraws all returns each year instead of reinvesting. What does she lose compared to compounding?

answer_text.en: Returns on her returns — the base never grows beyond the original principal
answer_text.ru: Доход на доход — база никогда не растёт выше первоначальной суммы
answer_text.kk: Returns on her returns — the base never grows beyond the original principal

answer_text.en: Nothing — 10% per year is the same whether compounded or not
answer_text.ru: Ничего — 10% в год одинаковы со сложным процентом и без него
answer_text.kk: Nothing — 10% per year is the same whether compounded or not

answer_text.en: Only the tax benefit of compounding
answer_text.ru: Только налоговую выгоду сложного процента
answer_text.kk: Only the tax benefit of compounding

answer_text.en: Half her total return over the period
answer_text.ru: Половину всей доходности за период
answer_text.kk: Half her total return over the period

description.en: Divide 72 by any annual rate to estimate how long it takes to double. A fast mental model that works for growth and for debt.
description.ru: Разделите 72 на годовую ставку, чтобы оценить, сколько времени нужно для удвоения. Быстрая мысленная модель, которая работает и для роста, и для долга.
description.kk: Divide 72 by any annual rate to estimate how long it takes to double. A fast mental model that works for growth and for debt.

title.en: How long to double your money?
title.ru: Сколько времени нужно, чтобы удвоить деньги?
title.kk: How long to double your money?

body.en: Compound growth makes precise projections complex. But one shortcut gives you an instant answer for any rate: divide 72 by the annual rate, and you get the approximate number of years to double. No calculator needed.
body.ru: Сложный рост делает точные прогнозы сложными. Но один приём даёт быстрый ответ для любой ставки: разделите 72 на годовую ставку — и получите примерное число лет до удвоения. Калькулятор не нужен.
body.kk: Compound growth makes precise projections complex. But one shortcut gives you an instant answer for any rate: divide 72 by the annual rate, and you get the approximate number of years to double. No calculator needed.

tip.en: 72 ÷ rate = doubling time. This is accurate within 1–2 years for rates between 2% and 20%.
tip.ru: 72 ÷ ставка = время удвоения. Для ставок от 2% до 20% точность обычно в пределах 1–2 лет.
tip.kk: 72 ÷ rate = doubling time. This is accurate within 1–2 years for rates between 2% and 20%.

body.en: The rule comes from the math of logarithms applied to compound growth. The exact formula is ln(2) / ln(1 + r) ≈ 0.693 / r. Since 72 is close to 69.3 and divides cleanly by many common rates (2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12), it became the standard shortcut.
body.ru: Правило связано с математикой логарифмов в сложном росте. Точная формула: ln(2) / ln(1 + r) ≈ 0,693 / r. Число 72 близко к 69,3 и удобно делится на многие распространённые ставки (2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12), поэтому стало стандартным сокращением.
body.kk: The rule comes from the math of logarithms applied to compound growth. The exact formula is ln(2) / ln(1 + r) ≈ 0.693 / r. Since 72 is close to 69.3 and divides cleanly by many common rates (2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12), it became the standard shortcut.

example.en: At 9%: 72 ÷ 9 = 8 years to double. At 12%: 72 ÷ 12 = 6 years. At 6%: 72 ÷ 6 = 12 years. The exact answers are 8.0, 6.1, and 11.9 — the rule is very close.
example.ru: При 9%: 72 ÷ 9 = 8 лет до удвоения. При 12%: 72 ÷ 12 = 6 лет. При 6%: 72 ÷ 6 = 12 лет. Точные ответы — 8,0; 6,1; 11,9 — правило очень близко.
example.kk: At 9%: 72 ÷ 9 = 8 years to double. At 12%: 72 ÷ 12 = 6 years. At 6%: 72 ÷ 6 = 12 years. The exact answers are 8.0, 6.1, and 11.9 — the rule is very close.

title.en: The rule works for debt and inflation too
title.ru: Правило работает и для долга, и для инфляции
title.kk: The rule works for debt and inflation too

body.en: The same logic applies in reverse. If a microloan charges 36% annual interest (common in KZ), your debt doubles in 72 ÷ 36 = 2 years if unpaid. If inflation runs at 8%, prices double in 9 years. The rule quantifies how quickly both sides of the balance sheet move.
body.ru: Та же логика работает и в обратную сторону. Если микрокредит берёт 36% годовых (часто встречается в РК), неоплаченный долг удвоится за 72 ÷ 36 = 2 года. Если инфляция 8%, цены удвоятся за 9 лет. Правило показывает, как быстро движутся обе стороны личного баланса.
body.kk: The same logic applies in reverse. If a microloan charges 36% annual interest (common in KZ), your debt doubles in 72 ÷ 36 = 2 years if unpaid. If inflation runs at 8%, prices double in 9 years. The rule quantifies how quickly both sides of the balance sheet move.

tip.en: Use the rule offensively (how fast does my investment grow?) and defensively (how fast does my debt or inflation destroy value?).
tip.ru: Используйте правило и наступательно (как быстро растёт инвестиция?), и защитно (как быстро долг или инфляция уничтожают ценность?).
tip.kk: Use the rule offensively (how fast does my investment grow?) and defensively (how fast does my debt or inflation destroy value?).

title.en: Rate vs doubling time: the full picture
title.ru: Ставка и время удвоения: полная картина
title.kk: Rate vs doubling time: the full picture

blocks.text: Starting with 1,000,000 ₸, here is how long it takes to reach 2,000,000 ₸ at different rates, using Rule of 72 estimates.

Notice: doubling time does not shrink linearly with rate. Going from 6% to 12% (double the rate) cuts the doubling time in half — from 12 to 6 years. This is the power of rate differences in compounding.
Если начать с 1 000 000 ₸, вот сколько времени нужно, чтобы достичь 2 000 000 ₸ при разных ставках по оценке Правила 72.

Обратите внимание: время удвоения не уменьшается линейно со ставкой. Переход с 6% на 12% (удвоение ставки) сокращает время удвоения вдвое — с 12 до 6 лет. В этом сила различий в ставках при сложном проценте.
Starting with 1,000,000 ₸, here is how long it takes to reach 2,000,000 ₸ at different rates, using Rule of 72 estimates.

Notice: doubling time does not shrink linearly with rate. Going from 6% to 12% (double the rate) cuts the doubling time in half — from 12 to 6 years. This is the power of rate differences in compounding.

en.instruction: Each statement applies the Rule of 72. Find the one that contains a mistake.

cases.text: "At 8% annual return, my 500,000 ₸ will double to 1,000,000 ₸ in about 9 years."
options: a) No error — this is correct
b) Error — the doubling time is wrong
cases.explanation: 72 ÷ 8 = 9. Correct. At 8%, money doubles in approximately 9 years.

cases.text: "A loan at 24% annual interest will double the debt in about 6 years if nothing is repaid."
options: a) No error — this is correct
b) Error — the doubling time is wrong
cases.explanation: 72 ÷ 24 = 3 years, not 6. At 24%, debt doubles in 3 years — twice as fast as stated.

cases.text: "Inflation at 6% means prices will roughly double in 12 years."
options: a) No error — this is correct
b) Error — the doubling time is wrong
cases.explanation: 72 ÷ 6 = 12. Correct. At 6% inflation, the price level doubles in approximately 12 years.

en.final_explanation: The Rule of 72 is symmetrical: it applies equally to investment growth, debt growth, and inflation. A common mistake is misremembering the formula as '72 × rate' instead of '72 ÷ rate'.

body.en: The Rule of 72 converts an abstract rate into a concrete doubling period. This reframes financial decisions: a 12% investment isn't just '4% more than 9%' — it doubles in 6 years vs 8 years. That mental model connects naturally to investment horizon, which determines how many doublings you can realistically achieve.

description.en: How long you plan to stay invested shapes which assets make sense — longer horizons absorb volatility and let compounding do its work.
description.ru: How long you plan to stay invested shapes which assets make sense — longer horizons absorb volatility and let compounding do its work.
description.kk: How long you plan to stay invested shapes which assets make sense — longer horizons absorb volatility and let compounding do its work.

body.en: A volatile asset is only risky if you might need to sell it during a downturn. For money you won't touch for 20 years, short-term price swings are noise. For money you need in 6 months, they are a real threat. Your investment horizon is not a preference — it is a constraint that determines which assets are appropriate.
body.ru: A volatile asset is only risky if you might need to sell it during a downturn. For money you won't touch for 20 years, short-term price swings are noise. For money you need in 6 months, they are a real threat. Your investment horizon is not a preference — it is a constraint that determines which assets are appropriate.

tip.en: Ask: 'When will I actually need this money?' That answer defines your horizon.
tip.ru: Ask: 'When will I actually need this money?' That answer defines your horizon.

body.en: Short horizon (under 3 years): capital preservation is the goal — cash, deposits, short bonds. Medium (3–10 years): balance growth and stability. Long (10+ years): focus on growth; short-term volatility is acceptable because time smooths it out. Mixing assets from different horizon buckets creates mismatch risk.

title.en: Sequence risk: why timing of returns matters
title.ru: Sequence risk: why timing of returns matters

body.en: If markets crash just as you begin withdrawing (e.g. at retirement), you are forced to sell more units to raise the same cash — permanently depleting the base. This is sequence-of-returns risk. A long accumulation horizon is not affected by it; a short horizon near withdrawal is very exposed.

body.en: Before choosing an asset, know when you'll need the money. Horizon determines acceptable volatility, recovery runway, and the compounding window available to you. An investor who gets this right avoids the most common portfolio mistake: holding assets that are structurally wrong for their timeline. Dollar-cost averaging is the discipline that turns a long horizon into consistent action.

description.en: Invest a fixed amount on a regular schedule. You buy more when prices are low, fewer when high — automatically lowering your average cost over time.

body.en: Investing a large sum all at once feels risky — what if you buy right before a crash? Waiting for the 'right moment' means most investors sit in cash too long and miss gains. Dollar-cost averaging (DCA) sidesteps this by removing the timing decision entirely: invest a fixed amount on a fixed schedule, regardless of price.

title.en: How DCA lowers your average cost
title.ru: How DCA lowers your average cost
title.kk: How DCA lowers your average cost

body.en: When you invest a fixed amount, a lower price buys more units; a higher price buys fewer. This asymmetry works in your favour over time: you accumulate more units during dips than during peaks. Your average cost per unit ends up lower than the average price over the same period.

example.en: Invest 50,000 ₸/month for 3 months. Prices: 1,000 ₸, 500 ₸, 2,000 ₸. Units bought: 50, 100, 25 = 175 units for 150,000 ₸. Average cost: 857 ₸. Average price: 1,167 ₸. DCA paid 27% less per unit.

title.en: DCA vs lump sum: when each wins
title.ru: DCA vs lump sum: when each wins

body.en: Research consistently shows that lump-sum investing beats DCA about two-thirds of the time in rising markets — because money invested earlier compounds longer. DCA wins when markets fall or are highly volatile after the investment date. For most individuals, DCA's main value is behavioural: it makes starting easy and keeps you investing through downturns.

body.kk: Research consistently shows that lump-sum investing beats DCA about two-thirds of the time in rising markets — because money invested earlier compounds longer. DCA wins when markets fall or are highly volatile after the investment date. For most individuals, DCA's main value is behavioural: it makes starting easy and keeps you investing through downturns.

tip.en: If you have a lump sum and a long horizon, investing it all at once is usually optimal mathematically. But DCA beats not investing at all — always.
tip.ru: If you have a lump sum and a long horizon, investing it all at once is usually optimal mathematically. But DCA beats not investing at all — always.
tip.kk: If you have a lump sum and a long horizon, investing it all at once is usually optimal mathematically. But DCA beats not investing at all — always.

blocks.text: Azat invests 30,000 ₸ every month for 6 months. Fund price fluctuates:

Total invested: 180,000 ₸. Average cost per unit: 180,000 ÷ 145.8 = 1,235 ₸. Average price over 6 months: (1,500+1,200+900+1,100+1,400+1,600) ÷ 6 = 1,283 ₸. DCA cost was 4% lower than simply averaging the prices — because more units were purchased during the low months.

en.instruction: Evaluate each investor's approach. Does it follow good DCA practice or undermine it?
options: Good DCA practice
Undermines DCA

scenarios.text: Bekzat sets up an automatic transfer of 25,000 ₸ into an index fund on the 1st of every month and never changes it.
scenarios.explanation: Automation removes emotion. Fixed amount, fixed schedule, consistent execution — this is textbook DCA.

scenarios.text: Sholpan invests her 25,000 ₸ only in months when the market went up the previous month, skipping months after drops.
scenarios.explanation: Skipping purchases after drops eliminates the key benefit of DCA — buying more units when prices are low. She buys only at higher prices.

scenarios.text: Timur doubles his monthly contribution to 50,000 ₸ during a 30% market drawdown because he sees it as a discount.
scenarios.explanation: Increasing contributions during drawdowns is consistent with DCA logic — lower prices mean more units per tenge. It enhances, not undermines, the strategy.

body.en: Dollar-cost averaging is where the concepts from this topic converge: it forces regular investment (beating inflation), reinvests over time (enabling compounding), and works regardless of short-term prices (matching your horizon). The discipline is not the strategy — it is the delivery mechanism that makes every other principle work in practice.

question_text.en: Why does investing a fixed tenge amount regularly result in a lower average cost per unit than the average price over the same period?
question_text.ru: Why does investing a fixed tenge amount regularly result in a lower average cost per unit than the average price over the same period?
question_text.kk: Why does investing a fixed tenge amount regularly result in a lower average cost per unit than the average price over the same period?

answer_text.en: Because fixed amounts buy more units at low prices and fewer at high prices, weighting the average toward cheaper purchases
answer_text.ru: Because fixed amounts buy more units at low prices and fewer at high prices, weighting the average toward cheaper purchases
answer_text.kk: Because fixed amounts buy more units at low prices and fewer at high prices, weighting the average toward cheaper purchases

answer_text.en: Because regular investing unlocks discounted prices from brokers
answer_text.ru: Because regular investing unlocks discounted prices from brokers
answer_text.kk: Because regular investing unlocks discounted prices from brokers

answer_text.en: Because DCA filters out the highest-price months automatically
answer_text.ru: Because DCA filters out the highest-price months automatically
answer_text.kk: Because DCA filters out the highest-price months automatically

answer_text.en: Because cost averaging is only calculated on months where prices fell

question_text.en: A bank deposit yields 8% and inflation is 9.5%. After 5 years, what has happened to the real value of the money in that account?
question_text.ru: A bank deposit yields 8% and inflation is 9.5%. After 5 years, what has happened to the real value of the money in that account?
question_text.kk: A bank deposit yields 8% and inflation is 9.5%. After 5 years, what has happened to the real value of the money in that account?

answer_text.en: It has fallen — the nominal balance grew but purchasing power declined by roughly 7% total
answer_text.ru: It has fallen — the nominal balance grew but purchasing power declined by roughly 7% total
answer_text.kk: It has fallen — the nominal balance grew but purchasing power declined by roughly 7% total

answer_text.en: It has grown — 8% annual return always increases real wealth
answer_text.ru: It has grown — 8% annual return always increases real wealth
answer_text.kk: It has grown — 8% annual return always increases real wealth

answer_text.en: It is unchanged — deposits are inflation-protected by law in Kazakhstan

answer_text.kk: It is unchanged — deposits are inflation-protected by law in Kazakhstan

answer_text.en: It has fallen by exactly 1.5% per year, totalling 7.5% nominal loss

answer_text.kk: It has fallen by exactly 1.5% per year, totalling 7.5% nominal loss

question_text.en: Investor A starts with 300,000 ₸ at age 22. Investor B starts with 900,000 ₸ at age 38. Both earn 10%/year and retire at 62. Who has more, and what explains the difference?
question_text.ru: Investor A starts with 300,000 ₸ at age 22. Investor B starts with 900,000 ₸ at age 38. Both earn 10%/year and retire at 62. Who has more, and what explains the difference?
question_text.kk: Investor A starts with 300,000 ₸ at age 22. Investor B starts with 900,000 ₸ at age 38. Both earn 10%/year and retire at 62. Who has more, and what explains the difference?

answer_text.en: A has more — 40 years of compounding on a smaller base outpaces 24 years on a larger one
answer_text.ru: A has more — 40 years of compounding on a smaller base outpaces 24 years on a larger one
answer_text.kk: A has more — 40 years of compounding on a smaller base outpaces 24 years on a larger one

answer_text.en: B has more — tripling the starting amount always outweighs the time advantage
answer_text.ru: B has more — tripling the starting amount always outweighs the time advantage
answer_text.kk: B has more — tripling the starting amount always outweighs the time advantage

answer_text.en: They end up roughly equal because B's larger principal compensates exactly for A's longer horizon
answer_text.ru: They end up roughly equal because B's larger principal compensates exactly for A's longer horizon
answer_text.kk: They end up roughly equal because B's larger principal compensates exactly for A's longer horizon

answer_text.en: A has more only if markets deliver above-average returns during his early years

answer_text.kk: A has more only if markets deliver above-average returns during his early years

question_text.en: A microloan in Kazakhstan charges 48% annual interest. Using the Rule of 72, how long before an unpaid 200,000 ₸ debt reaches 800,000 ₸?

question_text.kk: A microloan in Kazakhstan charges 48% annual interest. Using the Rule of 72, how long before an unpaid 200,000 ₸ debt reaches 800,000 ₸?

answer_text.en: About 3 years — debt doubles in 1.5 years at 48%, so two doublings take 3 years
answer_text.ru: About 3 years — debt doubles in 1.5 years at 48%, so two doublings take 3 years
answer_text.kk: About 3 years — debt doubles in 1.5 years at 48%, so two doublings take 3 years

answer_text.en: About 6 years — 72 ÷ 48 = 1.5, and reaching 4× takes 4 doublings

answer_text.kk: About 6 years — 72 ÷ 48 = 1.5, and reaching 4× takes 4 doublings

question_text.en: Daniyar invests 40,000 ₸/month into an index fund. In March the fund drops 25%. His friend tells him to pause contributions and wait for recovery. What is the flaw in this advice?
question_text.ru: Daniyar invests 40,000 ₸/month into an index fund. In March the fund drops 25%. His friend tells him to pause contributions and wait for recovery. What is the flaw in this advice?
question_text.kk: Daniyar invests 40,000 ₸/month into an index fund. In March the fund drops 25%. His friend tells him to pause contributions and wait for recovery. What is the flaw in this advice?

answer_text.en: Pausing eliminates the DCA benefit — March's low price would buy more units, reducing his average cost
answer_text.ru: Pausing eliminates the DCA benefit — March's low price would buy more units, reducing his average cost
answer_text.kk: Pausing eliminates the DCA benefit — March's low price would buy more units, reducing his average cost

answer_text.en: Pausing is always correct — never invest during a downturn
answer_text.ru: Pausing is always correct — never invest during a downturn
answer_text.kk: Pausing is always correct — never invest during a downturn

answer_text.en: Pausing makes no difference — DCA works the same whether you skip months or not
answer_text.ru: Pausing makes no difference — DCA works the same whether you skip months or not
answer_text.kk: Pausing makes no difference — DCA works the same whether you skip months or not

answer_text.en: The flaw is that Daniyar should switch to a lump-sum approach instead
answer_text.ru: The flaw is that Daniyar should switch to a lump-sum approach instead
answer_text.kk: The flaw is that Daniyar should switch to a lump-sum approach instead
